notes: Edges are derived; weight = number of distinct trials linking the two endpoints. Each edge carries two citations, one per endpoint field_path (G-25).

Drug nodes are keyed by active ingredient: names are normalized (case, punctuation, dose/strength, salt, and dose-form/route stripped — 'Sunitinib Malate'≡'Sunitinib', 'Selumetinib, 100mg'≡'Selumetinib'), then a brand folds into its generic via an alias-only synonym merge — an otherName merges two drugs ONLY when it is itself another drug's primary name (Keytruda↔pembrolizumab) AND that alias is corroborated by ≥2 trials, so a single-trial registry mislabel can't collapse two drugs. A combination product ('Drug A + Drug B') never merges its components (CC-12). Conservative tradeoff: a brand/generic pair attested by only ONE trial stays split (the safe direction).
Placebo / standard-of-care interventions are excluded by name to avoid a false mega-hub (G-36).

Nodes capped at the top 60 by degree; edges below weight 2 are pruned (only pairs co-occurring in ≥2 trials are shown). This is a configurable default optimizing interpretability, not maximal edge inclusion — the underlying co-occurrence graph is unchanged.
